text.title: User Control Rules, Accessibility, and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Concrete audit rules, accessibility requirements for control mechanisms, common mistakes, and agent limitations.

rule.statement: Every user-facing action must be assessed for: undo availability, cancel availability, exit mechanism, and recovery method.
rule.rationale: Missing any one of these for a high-impact action is a usability defect; systematic audit prevents gaps.

rule.statement: Flag high-severity gaps as: irreversible action with no confirmation AND no undo.
rule.rationale: The combination of irreversibility and lack of both undo and confirmation is the highest-risk pattern; it must be addressed before shipping.

section.title: Accessibility Requirements
section.p: Keyboard shortcuts for undo (Ctrl+Z) and redo (Ctrl+Y/Shift+Z) must be implemented. Escape key must always close modals and dismiss overlays. Focus must return to the triggering element when a modal closes. Undo actions must be announced to screen readers. Cancel and close buttons must have text labels, not icon-only affordances.

antipattern.description: No undo for frequently performed actions.
antipattern.reason: Users who make frequent mistakes in high-volume workflows lose trust quickly when recovery requires more effort than the original action.

antipattern.description: Hidden or hard-to-find Cancel buttons in forms and dialogs.
antipattern.reason: Users who cannot find the exit feel trapped; Cancel must be visually prominent and near the primary action button.

antipattern.description: Confirmation dialog overuse — "Are you sure?" on every action including non-destructive ones.
antipattern.reason: Confirmation fatigue causes users to dismiss dialogs without reading, defeating their purpose for high-stakes actions.

rule.statement: Pair agent audit with axe-core or pa11y run; agent cannot detect focus traps in rendered interfaces.
rule.rationale: Agent identifies structural absence of controls from descriptions; actual keyboard navigation testing requires browser tooling.

rule.statement: Undo and cancel implementation decisions require engineering sign-off; agent audit identifies the need, not the technical approach.
rule.rationale: Undo architecture depends on backend event sourcing, transaction logs, or soft-delete patterns that require engineering judgment on consistency and rollback scope.

text.reference: Playwright test to verify Escape key closes modals
